text:
Nugui (ヌグイ, Nugui) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 is the personal butler for King Nasubi who administered the Seed Urn Ceremony to each of the Princes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 

Appearance

Nugui is a very petite man of elderly age. He has well-combed hair, a square jaw, a bulbous nose, low cheekbones, and long earlobes. He dresses in a black suit, a white dress shirt, with a laced tie. 

Personality

Nugui keeps a calm and professional attitude at all times, and is often seen smiling politely. Nonetheless, he displayed sorrow when Nasubi was in front of his daughter's grave, and expressed his condolences to the king. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Nugui makes his first appearance in the story when he goes to 4 th Prince Tserriednich Hui Guo Rou to inform him of the Succession Contest and its conditions for participating. While standing in front of a box half the size of his small stature, Nugui tells Tserriednich that participation is limited to the children of legal wives of King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou that also board the Black Whale and participate in its departure ceremony. 

Tserriednich asks Nugui how many people plan to enter, but hears Nugui apologize and inform him that he could not tell him until the ceremony, and that the contest would be canceled if any candidate dies before the ceremony. Nugui adds that the contest would officially begin the moment the Black Whale finishes sounding its departure horn, and that once it begins, the right to succession of the winner would not be questioned no matter the measures taken to outlast the other contestants. 

As he opens the box and looks at the mysterious urn within, Nugui states that the sole survivor will be the official heir to the throne. He pulls the urn out, holding it to face Tserriednich, and tells Tserriednich to, if he agrees to the terms, offer a drop of blood to the urn and then insert his hand into its center. He holds the urn up for Tserriednich to drop a drop of blood into the urn, then instructs Tserriednich to insert his hand into its mouth-like orifice, reassuring Tserriednich that it would not bite. 

Nugui watches as Tserriednich completes the ceremony, then tells him that that would be do. He explains the ceremony as part of the Kakin Royal Family tradition, and that it is believed that one would receive a special power by proving their inheritance to the urn with blood, and focusing on their desire for the crown. 

During the banquet on the first day of the voyage, Nugui is seen informing the Princes of their time to retire, first seen informing 8 th Queen Oito that the time for her and 14 th Prince Woble to retire had come, and after some time informing 10 th Prince Kacho that it was her time to retire. When he goes to inform Kacho to leave, she calls 11 th Prince Fugetsu to leave with her, and Nugui reminds her that only one Prince could leave at a time. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 After some more time, he goes to inform 9 th Prince Halkenburg, and is slightly confused to see Halkenburg heading in an unexpected direction. He hears Halkenburg say that he wants to approach his father. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 361 

On the second day, Nugui stands behind Nasubi as Nasubi observes 12 th Prince Momoze's casket and pays his respects. With a sorrowful look on his face, Nugui offers his condolences to Nasubi. 

On the sixth day, Halkenburg breaks into his father's chamber in an attempt to force him to end the contest at gunpoint. Nugui enters the chamber, escorted by three chambers, and informs Halkenburg that their current circumstances had been accounted for before the ship's departure as secret training exercises, and that neither Halkenburg nor his guards that helped him would be punished. 

On the eighth day, at night during the Sunday banquet, Nugui discreetly approaches Princes Kacho and Fugetsu at their table to explain and apologize for the changes in the banquet program and the reason for those changes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383
